paper.title: On the Structural Non-Preservation of Epistemic Behaviour under Policy Transformation
paper.abstract: Reinforcement learning (RL) agents under partial observability often condition actions on internally accumulated information such as memory or inferred latent context. We formalise such information-conditioned interaction patterns as behavioural dependency: variation in action selection with respect to internal information under fixed observations. This induces a probe-relative notion of $ε$-behavioural equivalence and a within-policy behavioural distance that quantifies probe sensitivity. We es

memory_insight.before_after_comparison: Before: 2-4 GB, After: 1-2 GB (under convex aggregation)

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Gradient ascent on a skewed mixture objective can decrease behavioural distance when a dominant-mode gradient aligns with the direction of steepest contraction.

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
